tasks.title: Complete Button/Input/FormField primitive normalization in Knit
tasks.description: Finish Stage 1 of the Knit primitive replacement wave. The remaining high-use Knit flows in SidebarContent and settings still need to adopt Looma Button, Input, Select, and FormField primitives. This is listed as 'in progress' in the migration inventory.
outOfScope: New Looma components not already shipped

acceptanceCriteria.description: SidebarContent.vue uses Looma Button, Input, Select, FormField in all dialog-heavy flows
settings.vue uses Looma primitives for all form controls
pnpm typecheck passes in Knit
pnpm build passes in Knit
looma-migration-inventory.md updated to reflect completed status for affected surfaces

tasks.title: Promote AvatarGroup from Knit to Looma
tasks.description: Extract the AvatarGroup/AvatarStack pattern from Knit's atoms/AvatarGroup.vue into a generic Looma component. Presence-specific data binding must stay in Knit; only the generic presentation layer moves to Looma. Per the migration inventory this is a B-category promotion candidate.
outOfScope: Any Knit-specific avatar naming or workspace vocabulary in the API

acceptanceCriteria.description: Generic ui-avatar-group component ships in @looma/core with domain-neutral API (no workspace/presence vocabulary)
React and Vue adapter exports exist for AvatarGroup
Contract README, Docusaurus page, and Storybook story all ship in the same change
pnpm generate:api && pnpm check:docs-sync pass
Knit's AvatarGroup.vue is updated to consume the new Looma primitive

tasks.title: Fix open editor bugs blocking Knit dogfooding
tasks.description: There are open shared editor defects found through Knit dogfooding documented in looma/docs/editor-bugs.md. These are blocking broader editor UI migration. Read editor-bugs.md and fix the listed defects in @looma/editor.
outOfScope: New editor features not in editor-roadmap.md

acceptanceCriteria.description: Each bug listed in docs/editor-bugs.md is either fixed or explicitly deferred with rationale
pnpm --filter @looma/editor typecheck passes
pnpm --filter @looma/editor build passes
docs/editor-bugs.md updated with fix status for each item

tasks.title: Implement inline comments — schema exists, API + UI to do
tasks.description: The comments table already exists in the Supabase schema. This task implements the API endpoints and UI for inline/page comments: select text → add comment, thread replies, show comments on page. This is item #5 in the Knit feature roadmap parity list.
outOfScope: Any changes to the comments table schema

acceptanceCriteria.description: User can select text in the editor and add a comment
Comments appear in a sidebar or inline indicator on the page
Thread replies work (add reply, display thread)
server/api routes for comment CRUD exist and are protected by workspace membership
pnpm typecheck passes

tasks.title: Add unit tests for use-collections, use-presence, and subdomain utils
tasks.description: V1 polish item from PROJECT_STATE.md. These three composables/utils have no unit tests. Write vitest tests covering the main behaviours: collection tree reactivity, presence channel lifecycle, and subdomain slug extraction/construction.

acceptanceCriteria.description: use-collections has tests covering: initial load, create, optimistic reorder, and revert on error
subdomain utils has tests covering: extractWorkspaceSlug and workspaceHost for valid, invalid, and edge-case inputs
use-presence has tests covering: channel join, presence sync, and leave
pnpm test passes

tasks.title: Generate TypeScript types from Supabase schema
tasks.description: Run pnpm db:types to generate proper TypeScript types from the Supabase schema and wire them into the codebase. Currently Knit uses ad-hoc types. This is a V1 polish item and will improve type safety across all server API routes and composables.
outOfScope: Migrating all files to use generated types in one pass (incremental adoption is fine)

acceptanceCriteria.description: Generated types file exists at the expected output path after running pnpm db:types
At least the server/api and composables directories import from generated types where the types overlap with existing ad-hoc types
pnpm typecheck passes after adoption
